system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        DICGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

vertices
(

    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2

    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight section (extended in +x)

    (  75  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  75 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  75 -125  25 )   // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x=-50 to x=0)
    hex (0 4 7 3 1 5 6 2) (20 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section (x=25 to x=75)
    hex (15 14 13 12 11 10 9 8) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }

        faces
        (

            (4 8 9 5)

        );

            (7 11 10 6)
            (11 15 14 10)
        );
    }

    leftWall

        faces

            (0 4 7 3)
            (4 7 6 5)
            (8 11 10 9)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {

        faces
        (
            (9 10 14 13)
        );
    }
);

(
);

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoSimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (15 14 13 12 11 10 9 8) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/squareBend/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 47.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors (Persistent Across All Attempts)

Two errors continue to appear in every attempt:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Various blocks are consistently "inside-out" or have "inward-pointing faces". The error message specifically identifies:
   ```
   Block hex (15 14 13 12 11 10 9 8) ... is inside-out
   ```
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" (cascading from blockMesh failure)

The blockMeshDict has now reached **Attempt 8** with no resolution. Six different vertex orderings and block topologies have been tried. The error message now correctly identifies Block 2 (`hex (15 14 13 12 11 10 9 8)`) as inside-out, which is the outlet block spanning vertices 8-15 from x=25 to x=75.

## Root Cause: The Block 2 Vertex Ordering Is Fundamentally Flawed

The current Block 2 is defined as:
```
hex (15 14 13 12 11 10 9 8)
```

Vertices:
- Bottom face (15, 14, 13, 12): (25, -125, 25), (25, -125, -25), (75, -125, -25), (75, -125, 25) — at y=-125
- Top face (11, 10, 9, 8): (25, -50, 25), (25, -50, -25), (75, -50, -25), (75, -50, 25) — at y=-50

The bottom face normal (using 15→14 and 15→12): 15→14 = (50, 0, 0)[+x], 15→12 = (50, 0, -50). Cross(+x, (50,0,-50)) = (0, 0, -2500)[-z].

The top face normal (11→10 and 11→9): 11→10 = (50, 0, 0)[+x], 11→9 = (50, 0, -50). Cross(+x, (50,0,-50)) = (0, 0, -2500)[-z].

**Both faces have normals pointing in the -z direction**, meaning they are parallel and facing the same direction — this is geometrically impossible for a hex block. The block needs two faces with normals pointing in OPPOSITE directions.

## Proposed Solution: Reverse the Entire Hex Vertex Ordering for Block 2

The fix is to swap the entire block so the top and bottom faces are reversed:

```
hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)
```

Now:
- Bottom face (8, 9, 10, 11) at y=-50: 8→9 = (0, 0, -50)[-z], 8→10 = (50, 0, -50). Cross(-z, (50,0,-50)) = (0, 0, -2500)[-z].
- Top face (12, 13, 14, 15) at y=-125: 12→13 = (50, 0, -50), 12→15 = (50, 0, 0). Cross((50,0,-50), +x) = (0, 0, -2500)[-z].

Still the same issue — both normals point -z. The problem is that the **faces (8,9,10,11) and (12,13,14,15) are both at constant-y planes**, meaning they are parallel to each other but the block spans in y, not in z. The faces should be at constant-x (the inlet and outlet of the block), not at constant-y.

**The correct Block 2 definition** must have faces that are offset in the **x-direction** (the block's height). The bottom face should be at x=25 (vertices 8,11,10,9) and the top face at x=75 (vertices 12,13,14,15):

```
hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)
```

Verify:
- Bottom face (8, 11, 10, 9) at x=25: 8(25,0,25)→11(25,-50,25)=(0,-50,0)[-y], 8→10=(50,-50,0). Cross(-y, (50,-50,0)) = (0, 0, -2500)[-z].
- Top face (12, 15, 14, 13) at x=75: 12(75,-125,25)→15(75,-125,-25)=(0,0,-50)[-z], 12→13=(0,50,0)[+y]. Cross(-z, +y) = +x.

Different normals (‑z vs +x) — still invalid.

**The only geometrically valid approach** is to recognize that the block spans from x=25 to x=75, with the two faces being at x=25 and x=75. The face at x=25 uses vertices (8, 9, 10, 11) and the face at x=75 uses vertices (12, 13, 14, 15). The correct ordering is:

```
hex (9 10 11 8 13 14 15 12)
```

Bottom face (9, 10, 11, 8): 9→10 = (50, 0, 0)[+x], 9→11 = (0, -50, 0)[-y]. Cross(+x, -y) = +z.
Top face (13, 14, 15, 12): 13→14 = (0, -50, 0)[-y], 13→12 = (-50, 0, 0)[-x]. Cross(-y, -x) = -z.

Opposite normals (+z vs -z) ✓ — this is a valid hex.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **In `blockMeshDict`**: Change Block 2 from `hex (15 14 13 12 11 10 9 8)` to `hex (9 10 11 8 13 14 15 12)`.

2. **Update all boundary face definitions** to match this new block topology.

3. **After blockMesh succeeds**, `rhoSimpleFoam` will find the mesh automatically.

4. **Verify boundary patch names** match across all `0/` field files post-mesh-generation.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change Block 2 hex definition from hex (15 14 13 12 11 10 9 8) to hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) with same grading (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); This ensures bottom face is 8 9 10 11 at y=-50 and top face is 12 13 14 15 at y=-125 with normals both pointing -z which is valid for a block extending in y-direction'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
